steps.title: Isolate the fraction containing n
steps.content:
Start by moving $-\frac{4}{t}$ to the right side:
$$\frac{20}{n} = -\frac{4}{w} + \frac{4}{t}$$
This groups the terms we can combine.

steps.title: Combine the right side over a common denominator
steps.content:
The right side has denominators $w$ and $t$, so use $tw$ as the common denominator:
$$-\frac{4}{w} + \frac{4}{t} = \frac{-4t}{tw} + \frac{4w}{tw} = \frac{4w - 4t}{tw} = \frac{4(w-t)}{tw}$$
Now we have: $$\frac{20}{n} = \frac{4(w-t)}{tw}$$

steps.title: Invert both sides and simplify
steps.content:
When $\frac{20}{n} = \frac{4(w-t)}{tw}$, take reciprocals of both sides:
$$\frac{n}{20} = \frac{tw}{4(w-t)}$$
Multiply both sides by 20:
$$n = \frac{20tw}{4(w-t)} = \frac{5tw}{w-t}$$
That is choice D, the correct answer.
